---
title: "ESSMS"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
    fig_caption: yes
bibliography: ../REFERENCES.bib
link-citations: true
reference-section-title: References
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{ESSMS}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
---
# Overview
```{r model_family_header, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_header(
  family = "essms",
  pages = c(
    Overview = "index.html",
    Implementation = "essms-implementation.html",
    Theory = "essms-theory.html"
  )
)
```


These pages are grounded in the classical elastic-shell scattering literature for fluid-filled spherical shells [@goodman_reflection_1962; @faran_sound_1951; @stanton_sound_1990].

```{r essms_overview, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_overview(
  family = "essms",
  summary = paste(
    "The elastic shelled spherical modal series (`ESSMS`) is the package's",
    "layered spherical family for an elastic shell surrounding either a fluid",
    "interior or a pressure-release cavity."
  ),
  core_idea = paste(
    "Represent the exterior acoustic field, the shell's longitudinal and",
    "transverse elastic fields, and the interior acoustic field in spherical",
    "modes, then match pressure, displacement, and traction conditions at both",
    "shell interfaces."
  ),
  best_for = c(
    "Spherical elastic shells with fluid or gas interiors",
    "Layered shell problems where shell resonances matter explicitly",
    "Theoretical reference work on spherical shell scattering"
  ),
  supports = c(
    "`ESS` spherical geometries",
    "Exterior seawater (medium `1`), shell (medium `2`), and interior fluid or cavity (medium `3`)",
    "Pressure-release and fluid-filled shell interiors"
  ),
  assumptions = c(
    "Perfectly spherical shell geometry",
    "Homogeneous isotropic shell material",
    "Linear elasticity and linear acoustics",
    "Modal truncation of an exact spherical layered system"
  ),
  reading = c(
    "[Implementation](essms-implementation.html): current usage and validation status",
    "[Theory](essms-theory.html): full layered shell derivation, tractions, and mode-wise boundary systems"
  )
)
```
